Richard | 1 reply I received a call from 678-405-9286.  A woman named Mary with a foreign accent (perhaps from India or Asia) attempted to persuade me into making an appointment to have my "supply bins and furnace cleaned".  They would be able to come to the house the next day.  It would only cost $34.95.  She gave me a number to call if interested.  You guess it, the number was 770-545-6012.  She also provided me with a website: www.cleaningbrothers.tk .  I pumped her for as much information as I could and then asked her to take my number off the list.  I smelled a scam to bilk Americans by getting their credit card information. Went to the website.  The .tk was suspicious and there is no physical address on the site.  Sure enough, according to Wikipedia, ".tk is the Internet country code top-level domain (ccTLD) for Tokelau, a territory of New Zealand located in the South Pacific."  Called the numbers back.  770-545-6012.  A man with a foreign accent answered.  He said the company was located on Chattahoochee Avenue in Atlanta, Georgia.  I could not find any business by that name at that location.  Called the 678-405-9286 number back that initially appeared on my caller ID.  Tried it several times but it would never connect.  They're a cleaning company, alright.  They'll clean out your credit card account.

HG (not) wells Received a call from this number to my husband’s cell phone. American sounding voice claimed to be Alicia Green from “tax review relief and support office”.  She was following up because my “account remains open for review and there is limited opportunity to address unresolved items before options narrow further.  We haven’t connected yet and waiting could mean fewer options later. Our team is assisting individuals with filing updates, penalty relief and balance adjustment that are only open during this period. This is a brief eligibility call to determine what can still be done.” She gives a direct line for calling her back at 202-428-0593 then asks us to call her at 888-691-1903. Press 2 to be removed if you no longer wish to be contacted. We have no idea who these people are and have no “accounts” with the at all nor did they ask for anyone by name. A little research shows this is a variant of other very similar sounding names using the exact same vagueness to get people to call them back.  There is no online presence for this entity. It is most likely a scam.

DALA | 1 reply BEWARE This is a clearinghouse that operates using multiple names, and not an individual. They have (so far) twice called our retail floral shop, and been dishonest on multiple levels.  The first BIG red flag is that they pretend to be an individual sender, but give multiple names that don't match.  If you corner them and ask "are you a company?" or "what is your company name?" the party on the phone will refuse to answer or lie.  Secondly they have asked to be tax exempt which is a HUGE red flag, because they will not provide a tax exemption certificate. They may call you from 323-489-6040.  The callback numbers they will provide are 855-874-4751 and -4763.  If you call either of them, you will hear a "bloop" as you are forwarded to an answering service at 916-538-3555

If a voice on the telephone claims you owe a debt: 1.    Demand the person give you his or her name, company name, and address 2.     Demand a debt validation letter by US mail.  E-mail no good. 3.    Do not pay  the collection agent or anyone else a  dime until the debt is validated Of course they won’t do this, no scammer ever will, so simply ignore them and block their calls. If they are bothering you, report them: http://www.consumer.ftc.gov/articles/0149-debt-collection (Threatening civil proceedings with no intent to follow through, or threatening criminal process in order to collect a civil debt is a  violation of the Federal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act.)  The same goes for endless telephone harassment

Shane Call received at 6:51 PM on 11/6/2025, call was rejected based on area code.  As expected for non-Utah area codes, I received an alert on my smartphone regarding the voicemail within the next minute.  This makes the 51st call left from this particular system since August 18th & the 61st loan scam call since May 7th this year (not including calls from previous years). While I can’t give the entirety of the message they intended for me to hear (given it starts reading off its script before the voicemail is ready to record), I can dictate from the re-recorded portion of what did get recorded to voicemail.  Their automated system stated: “…with personal loan underwriting.  I just wanted to check in with you about your personal loan file.  It’s already been reviewed by a senior underwriter and from what I see, everything looks good.  You’re nearly approved for $60,000 but, before we can move forward with funding, I just need to confirm your income detail real quick.  If you’re available, press 2 now to connect with me or someone from underwriting or give us a quick callback at 844-509-2754.  I’ll be her until 6 PM today and back tomorrow at 8 AM.  To stop future calls, just press 7.” RED FLAGS: 1.  I know better than to apply for a loan I can’t pay back & a legitimate loan company would know better than to approve a loan without sufficient income to pay it back. 2.  A legitimate loan company wouldn’t continue spoofing numbers to avoid getting blocked or incarcerated when their malicious actions are reported to legal authorities. 3.  A legitimate loan company wouldn’t continue calling nearly every day (with the exception of Sundays & certain holidays) for almost a month & a half when they aren't getting a response; they would have just closed out any loan application made. As always, these calls are reported to my cell provider, blocked (although blocks don’t really do any good when they call from a different spoofed number every time they call) & the voicemails are re-recorded when possible.  Those numbers & re-recorded voicemails also get published to a public online blacklist when I have time to update my personal site (along with links to sites like this when comments are available that report this type of malicious activity from those numbers).

Laura So here is the deal -  I have received 8 calls from this number in the last week and today I actually received a recorded message that said : " Hello, This message is from American Express.  This is not the telemarketing sales call.  We are calling you to discuss an important matter.  Please return our call at 1-800-592-1160".  The message then goes on to list the hours they are available.  The number is actually a valid AMEX number for the credit department, however, I called the main AMEX number and they transferred me to that department and they told me there is nothing missing or wrong with my account.  THIS IS A SCAM!!  Why, you ask, would someone trying to get my information, call me and provide a valid call back number?  This is to make you believe the call is real in the hopes that you will answer the call the next time and provide them with your credit card number and personal information.  If this is scam call, then why is the correct number showing up you ask?  It's a simple technique that they use called masking.  They just mask their number with the AMEX number so the AMEX number shows up on your caller ID.  Another reason I know it's a fake call is that they called me on my work mobile phone.  When I spoke to the credit dept, they asked me for a work phone number and a mobile phone number.  If they didn't have it on file, how would they have called me?  Another clue that this is fake, just look at the message.  It says "This is not the telemarketing sales call".  Dead give away that English is not their first language.  If it was, they would have said "This is not A telemarketing sales call" instead.  Uh yeah FRAUD!!  Additionally, if AMEX needs to get in touch with you, they will use multiple methods.  I receive notifications by email and not once did I get one saying they were trying to get in touch with me.  SO FOR THE LOVE OF GOD, DO NOT ANSWER THE PHONE AND DO NOT GIVE THEM ANY PERSONAL INFORMATION!!  THIS IS A SCAM!!

CWG40 Two hundred and forty-nine entries on this thread and I count about 110 as shill posts.   44% shill rate. The large number of  shillers basically posting to  tell everyone how "legit" this number is, is pretty strong evidence of a scam. This number is  most likely part of a larger fraud campaign targeting the customers of large-scale financial or commercial institutions. The large number of phony posts claiming the call is real,   is evidence of con artists spamming the site to try to fool people. At a 44% shill rate, there should be no question that this is part of a scam.  At least that's the way I see it based on my own research. I've seen percentages go as high as 50%. Sometimes even higher. This  scam is well documented.  Simply google it out.  Based  on my research this sort of scam has been going on since about 2008. T hese type of calls follow a  similar pattern.  They all  claim to be from groups like Chase, Citibank, Bank of America, Fifth Third Bank, US Bank, Wells Fargo, RBC, PayPal, etc as well as Credit Unions.  Or claim to be CC processors. Or “Fraud Detection”.   All with the same type of   postings.  All claiming there is something wrong with your card or that some questionable purchase has been made.  Or something similar.  They all ask for personal or personal financial information. Just part of the shillers  criminal MO. I've noticed the  shill posters often use the same turns of phrase.  Same old, same old verbiage telling  everyone how "legit" the calls are. Personally I think the group or groups  posting this shillage-spillage are located in either the US or Canada, or perhaps both.  They may also be working with scammers outside of North America. In any event , never give out any personal or financial information to anyone who cold calls you. Call history on 800notes for this type of scam, so far.  (That I’ve been able to find.) Anonym Got a call at my work phone from 1-410-965-1234 with caller ID "US GOVERNMENT." Seeing that caller ID threw an immediate red flag (yes, I'm naturally skeptical) because I've never communicated with any government agency from that phone nor given that number out. A quick glance at the number with a highly unlikely "1234" suffix made me send them to VM. Listened to a 14 second long VM of alien-like sounds - not the steady beep of a FAX misdirected to a phone or any human voice. Maybe the caller's prerecorded message machine is on the fritz, but all I got was distorted sounds - think Jimi Hendrix at the '67 Monterrey Pop Festival. Needless to say they won't be getting a call back. The posters who said something on the order of "it's definitely the Social Security Administration" or other government office, you are probably the people making these calls trying to legitimize your illegal misdeeds. If you're genuinely concerned it may the SSA calling about something important, I would highly recommend calling their national toll free number, 1-800-772-1213, and speak with an agent there to find out if they called you. But remember almost anything important is usually taken care of via postal mail unless you've specifically requested to be called. And as always, be very careful and very certain about who you are talking to before divulging any personal information.
